title: Hearty Canadian Beef Stew
description: A comforting, slow-cooked stew perfect for a chilly evening. This recipe showcases tender beef and fresh vegetables in a rich, flavorful broth.

ingredients.name: Fresh beef stew meat (1.5 lbs)
ingredients.notes: Choose a cut with good marbling for tenderness

ingredients.name: Fresh carrots (2 medium)
ingredients.notes: Peel and chop into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: Fresh potatoes (2 large)
ingredients.notes: Peel and chop into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: Fresh thyme (2 sprigs)
ingredients.notes: Chop fresh thyme for added flavor

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the browned beef back to the pot, along with the bouillon cubes and thyme.
Pour in 4 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
